title: Human Fracture Analysis Test Cases
purpose: Provides realistic narrative test cases to evaluate signal detection, escalation logic, and explanatory coherence.

cases.title: Dignified separation with residual goodwill
cases.narrative: We are separating, but we both want the children to feel safe. I help with the move and she helps me with the paperwork. We do not insult each other, and we try to keep adult pain away from the kids.

cases.title: Children used as loyalty carriers
cases.narrative: I told the children who really destroyed this family, because they deserve to know the truth. I need them to understand what their mother has done.

cases.title: Financial pressure to prevent departure
cases.narrative: If she leaves, I will stop paying anything extra and make sure she realizes how impossible life is without me. Then maybe she will understand what she is giving up.

cases.title: Acute collapse and self-erasing guilt
cases.narrative: I ruined everything and I do not even feel worthy of being near my own family. I honestly do not know if I want to survive this.

cases.title: Obsessive interrogation about a third party
cases.narrative: I keep asking whether there is another man. I know the answer will destroy me, but she has to tell me every detail or I will never let this go.

cases.title: Bitterness hardening into identity
cases.narrative: I am done being decent. She took my life, and I want her to feel that loss for the rest of her days. That is the only justice left.

cases.title: Short shock phase with later return to children
cases.narrative: For a few weeks after the divorce talk, I could barely think beyond survival. But now I am rebuilding routines with the children and planning around their needs.

cases.title: Administrative cruelty hidden in practical language
cases.narrative: I am not angry. I just see no reason to answer her emails quickly, sign the papers soon, or make this easier than necessary.

cases.title: Continued belonging without possession
cases.narrative: We are no longer a couple, but we are still a family in another form. I do not own her, yet I will always remain responsible toward the children and respectful toward her.

cases.title: Villain narrative with no self-implication
cases.narrative: This whole thing is entirely her fault. I did absolutely nothing wrong, and the only problem here is her character.

cases.title: Strategic niceness masking control
cases.narrative: I am being incredibly kind and generous so she can see what she is losing. Sooner or later she will realize she belongs here with me.

cases.title: Severe cluster: coercion, child leverage, degrading speech
cases.narrative: She is a liar and a coward. I will make sure the children know it, and I will not make the move or the legal process easy for her.
